caveats: a: Using the supplied labor ratio would attribute separately classified management labor to the fund vehicle and materially misstate this construct.
rho: AI adoption by advisers, administrators, transfer agents, or distributors belongs to their operating industries unless an explicit bridge is justified.
e: This is a taxonomy-based structural zero for the stated lens, not a claim that the investment-fund ecosystem lacks service providers.
s5: Fund continuation, merger, or liquidation is not equivalent to operating-company survival.
q: Fund expense ratios and investment returns are not interchangeable with retained gross benefit at a service operator.
d5: Durable investor use of open-end funds does not establish demand for an in-scope service firm in this code.
o: Acquiring or governing a regulated fund vehicle is not equivalent to owner-operating an independent LMM service business.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Investors purchase and redeem fund shares at net asset value; the fund pools assets and earns investment or property income. The investment adviser is typically a separate entity compensated for managing the portfolio, so adviser fee revenue is not the fund vehicle's external-service revenue.
lens.excluded_activities: Open-end mutual funds and money-market mutual funds are excluded because they are investment vehicles, not external-customer service firms. Investment advisers, portfolio managers, administrators, distributors, custodians, and other fee-earning operators are excluded when they are separately classified service establishments.
lens.included_activities: Potentially included only if an establishment correctly classified in NAICS 525910 supplies recurring outsourced services to unrelated external customers. The official definition instead identifies legal entities and open-end funds that pool securities or other financial instruments and continuously issue and redeem shares.

caveats: Using the supplied labor ratio would attribute separately classified management labor to the fund vehicle and materially misstate this construct.
a.change_evidence: A verified census of correctly classified 525910 establishments that sell recurring services to unrelated customers, with role-level payroll and workflow data, would establish whether a qualifying population exists and permit estimation.
a.rationale: AI-addressable labor share is undefined for the frozen lens because 525910 consists of fund vehicles rather than qualifying external-service firms. The supplied compensation-to-receipts ratio does not cure the mismatch: Census states that establishments with employees dedicated to fund management are classified in NAICS 5239.

caveats: Durable investor use of open-end funds does not establish demand for an in-scope service firm in this code.
d5.change_evidence: A validated population of correctly classified external-service firms plus customer renewal, budget, and substitution data would support estimation.
d5.rationale: Five-year demand durability for a recurring outsourced-service offering is undefined because no such offering is part of the official 525910 activity set.

caveats: This is a taxonomy-based structural zero for the stated lens, not a claim that the investment-fund ecosystem lacks service providers.
e.change_evidence: A revised official classification definition or establishment-level evidence that a correctly classified 525910 entity sells recurring services to unrelated external customers would challenge the structural zero.
e.rationale: The Census definition is a structural boundary: 525910 contains open-end investment-fund legal entities, while employees devoted to managing funds are classified in NAICS 5239. SEC materials likewise distinguish the fund from the typically separate investment adviser. Consequently, the share of this code's establishments eligible under the external-customer service lens is zero.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 525910 ($1,176,918,903) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 525910 ($1,479,130,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.2102 (margins.json[52]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Financial Svcs. (Non-bank & Insurance) EBITDA/Sales 21.02%. Note: Damodaran reports 0.00% for banks (EBITDA not meaningful); bank codes inherit this non-bank default and are flagged ESTIMATE like all n_band values.); revenue/employee $852,524 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 525910); class <5: 238 firms, avg revenue/firm $815,466 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $171,411 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 49 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,481,980 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $521,712 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 16 firms, avg revenue/firm $12,499,500 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,627,395 -> IN $1-10M band; class 20-99: 15 firms, avg revenue/firm $25,155,133 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $5,287,609 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 5 firms, avg revenue/firm $36,602,400 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $7,693,824 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 10 firms, avg revenue/firm $40,310,100 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $8,473,183 -> IN $1-10M band; n_band = 46

caveats: Acquiring or governing a regulated fund vehicle is not equivalent to owner-operating an independent LMM service business.
o.change_evidence: Evidence on ownership, regulation, governance, customer concentration, and day-to-day operations from correctly classified external-service establishments would permit estimation.
o.rationale: Owner operability is undefined because the frozen lens identifies no qualifying service-company operating model within 525910; the fund is overseen by a board and typically managed by a separate adviser.

caveats: Fund expense ratios and investment returns are not interchangeable with retained gross benefit at a service operator.
q.change_evidence: Contract-level pricing, delivery-cost, and customer-retention evidence from correctly classified 525910 external-service providers would permit a value-capture estimate.
q.rationale: Gross-benefit retention cannot be mapped to the defined industry because a fund pools investor capital and pays separately organized advisers and other providers; it is not a firm selling the recurring service contemplated by the lens.

caveats: AI adoption by advisers, administrators, transfer agents, or distributors belongs to their operating industries unless an explicit bridge is justified.
rho.change_evidence: Documented deployments, implementation constraints, and adoption outcomes from correctly classified 525910 external-service establishments would make this primitive estimable.
rho.rationale: Realizable adoption is undefined because no qualifying outsourced-service workflow is identified within the official 525910 population.

caveats: Fund continuation, merger, or liquidation is not equivalent to operating-company survival.
s5.change_evidence: Evidence of qualifying correctly classified establishments, followed by longitudinal survival records for that population, would permit estimation.
s5.rationale: Five-year survival conditional on being an eligible firm is undefined because the eligible population is structurally empty under the frozen lens.

principal_driver: Official Census and SEC materials consistently separate the investment vehicle from the fee-earning adviser or manager.
principal_weakness: No establishment-level register was available to test for rare correctly classified hybrid operators, so the conclusion relies on official taxonomy and regulatory structure rather than an empirical enumeration.

sources.supports: Official definitions for subsector 525 and industry 525910, including fund-vehicle characteristics, continuous share issuance and redemption, absence of service-sale revenue, and classification of fund-management establishments in NAICS 5239.

sources.supports: Regulatory structure of open-end management companies, redeemable securities, board oversight, and the typically separate investment adviser that manages the portfolio for a fee.
sources.title: Investment Company Registration and Regulation Package

sources.supports: SEC overview of investment companies and open-end mutual funds as collective investment vehicles issuing redeemable shares.
